reason: The fix adds a standard sudoers directive (TYPE=sysadm_t ROLE=sysadm_r) via a file in /etc/sudoers.d, edited with visudo which validates syntax. This follows STIG hardening patterns for sudo and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage when applied correctly.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect sudoers syntax could impair sudo access if visudo is bypassed; Missing SELinux type/role definitions could cause permission denials for sudoed commands
